entry.title: Re: Can we filter for null values in Fiori elements?
entry.content: how you are sure the db is null? can you run a query against the db directly?

entry.title: Re: JV(Joint Venture) Fields for Profit Center
entry.content: Hi kumar , I have already posted my questions here. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/mdg-f-profit-center-replicate-joint-venture-fields/qaq-p/12113175/comment-id/4842613#M4842613 For which entity type you have added the joint venture fields?

entry.title: Add a walk in delegate to a class by External ID
entry.content:
Hi,

The "User ID" and "External ID" values in our Learning environment do not match in many cases. We have mapped "External ID" to their employee number and "User ID" is their SF generated number. As their employee number is widely recognised in the business, we'd like for Instructors to be able to add walk in delegates by being able to search for their "External ID" insttead of their "User ID".

Can this be configured, and if so how?

Bryan

entry.title: Re: Can't see Open Rejected Envelopes
entry.content:
Hi, I would suggest making sure the user experiencing this issue is part of the Recover Rejected Envelopes group in the correct realm. If you're in that group in Test but the event is in Prod you'll not see the correct option, for example. 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: xfp output of Adobe form to be converted pdf
entry.content: The PDF displays correctly via SP01, right? ABAP can run SP01 too (via CALL TRANSACTION).

entry.title: Re: How to merge DMS object (eg. PDF/JPEG) to an Adobe fo...
entry.content: Did you get the solution?

entry.title: Re: xfp output of Adobe form to be converted pdf
entry.content: If your question is how to display PDF right after generating it, you have all the parameters you need in FP_JOB_OPEN (PREVIEW, GETPDF, etc.)

entry.title: Re: Can we filter for null values in Fiori elements?
entry.content: Yes, the CDS data preview shows 00-00-0000. And if the filter is applied as = null, the correct rows are displayed.

entry.title: Re: How to integrate BTP DMS with SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello Malar,

Thank you for reaching out to us. Could you please provide more details about the issue you're encountering, including a screenshot and a brief explanation? This information will be helpful for further investigating and resolving the issue.

entry.title: Re: Questions regarding Sucessfactors - Common super domain activation
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites, it's reserved to issues with SAP Community, Support and so on. Better post to Technology Q&A (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions). Hopefully, an admin would fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

I am a senior student in major Management Information Systems. I have a direction to have a intern job regarding SAP. Therefore, it is very important for me to have as much as knowledge in this field. I also have completed some courses in learning.sap.com before and 1 TS410 certificate. Now I really want to have chances to get other certificates that will helps me have a reliable resume in employers' eyes.

Thank you,

Vy Nguyen

entry.title: SAPUI5 ODataListBinding.create duplicates entries
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

I am building an SAPUI5 application based on OData V2 build with SEGW.

I am facing a weird behavior: In a sap.m.Table my created entries are duplicated after submitting and refreshing.

The table is defined like this:

<Table
    id="KeyfToSourceTable"
    mode="{= ${keyfEdit>/editing} ? 'MultiSelect' : 'None' }"
    items="{
        path: '/MapKeyfSourceSet'
    }" >
    <headerToolbar>
        <OverflowToolbar>
        <Title text="Table" level="H3" />
        <ToolbarSpacer />
            <Button
                icon="sap-icon://add"
                press=".onDatasourceHelpRequested"
                visible="{keyfEdit>/editing}" />
            <Button
                icon="sap-icon://delete"
                press=".onDatasourceDeletePressed"
                visible="{keyfEdit>/editing}" />
        
        </OverflowToolbar>
    </headerToolbar>
    <columns>
        <Column>
            <Text text="Sourcestxt" />
        </Column>
        <Column>
            <Text text="Employeetext" />
        </Column>
        <Column>
            <Text text="SbTxtlg" />
        </Column>
    </columns>
    <items>
        <ColumnListItem vAlign="Middle">
            <cells>
                <ObjectIdentifier
                    title="{Sourcestxt}"/>
                <Text
                    text="{Employeetext}" />
                <Text
                    text="{SbTxtlg}" />
            </cells>
        </ColumnListItem>
    </items>
</Table>

It displays entities mapped to my main object. So I filter these at runtime (and sometimes I have to rebind the table):

var sourceSystemTable = this.byId("KeyfToSourceTable");
var keyfId = this.getView().getBindingContext().getProperty("Keyfid");
var filterArray = [new Filter("Keyfid", FilterOperator.EQ, keyfId)];
sourceSystemTable.bindItems({path:"/MapKeyfSourceSet", template: oldTemplate, filters: new Filter(filterArray, true)})

The User should be able to edit the main object and the mapping table and just save at the end. But the table should display the new entries, which are not persisted yet. Therefore I use the ODataListBinding.create function like it is discribed in the documentation:

this.byId("KeyfToSourceTable").getBinding("items").create(oEntry, {
    inactive : false,
    success: function(data) {
        alert("success createdMapping");
    },
    error: function(data) {
        alert("error createdMapping");
    }
});

It works as designed, new Entries are shown and not persisted until the user clicks "save" and submitChanges() is called.

But after a refresh() is called or the Binding of the table is changed, the new entries are duplicated. They are added at the top of the table again. I already looked at the BindingContexts of the items and the binding of the table, the bindings seem absolutly the same.

Even when I look at a differend main object and the filter doesn't seem to work for these duplicates. They also appear at the top of the table. But looking at the binding and also getting the filterted property, they shouldn't be there. These duplicates don't exists in the backend/data base tables. They also all are not transient, so I am not able to filter them by myself.

I assume these duplicates are just in the client model.

I appreciate any help,
Kind regards

Tobias

entry.title: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel
entry.content:
The SAP Analytics Cloud license includes two Microsoft Excel add-ins: the strategic SAP Analytics Cloud add-in for Microsoft Excel and SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition). The SAP Analytics Cloud add-in for Microsoft Excel is automatically updated and primarily focuses on the latest SAP solutions, like SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere. On the other hand, SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office excels at accessing SAP BW and BPC. This blog provides a comparison of their features and functionalities.

 

Installation

Both add-ins are integral components of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Enterprise subscription. To use them, end users must log in to their SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

The SAC Excel add-in is an Office add-in that offers cross-platform compatibility and centralized deployment and distribution. End users can directly install it from the Microsoft Store or, for centralized deployment, through the Microsoft 365 admin center. It supports Microsoft Excel 365 (Desktop and Web) and Microsoft Excel 2021 on Windows and macOS. Once installed, the "SAP Analytics Cloud" ribbon becomes visible within Excel. New features are automatically released to end users monthly, eliminating the need for dedicated installations. Patches may be deployed more frequently. The roadmap is available on SAP's roadmap explorer. For more information, please refer to the SAP Help and What's New pages.

The SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (AFO) (SAC edition) installer must be downloaded from the SAP Software Download Center and then installed on the end user's machine. Microsoft Excel 2016 (Desktop) or later is required. The Excel ribbon will display the "Analysis" and "Analysis Design" tabs. A new service pack with customer bug fixes is released every quarter. There are no plans for new features, and mainstream maintenance is scheduled to end in late 2029 (as per the Product Availability Matrix). There are two versions of Analysis for Office: the AFO SAC edition and the Standard Edition. This blog highlights the key differences between these two versions.

 

Workbook design and storage

After authenticating with SAP Analytics Cloud and inserting a cross-tab based on a data source, the side panel can be used in both add-ins to design the workbook. Measures can be filtered, and dimensions can be added or removed from the rows and columns of a cross-tab. Multiple cross-tabs with different data sources can be used within a single workbook in both add-ins. Cross-tabs can be copied and pasted to other sheets, and undo/redo functionality is supported. Additionally, all standard Microsoft Excel features can be applied to cross-tabs.

For the SAP Analytics Cloud Excel add-in, the primary focus is on cloud data sources. It always uses the connections defined in the SAP Analytics Cloud connection management. While SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office can utilize some SAP Analytics Cloud live connections, it also has the flexibility to connect directly to the source system.

The following table provides an overview of the most relevant SAP data sources:

Data sourceSAC Excel add-inAnalysis for Office (SAC edition)
SAP Analytics Cloud New ModelYesNo
SAP Datasphere Analytic ModelYesNo
SAP S/4HANA Public CloudYesYes
SAP HANA Cloud YesYes (HANA Analytics Adapter needed)
SAP BW and BW/4HANAYes (Tunnel connection)Yes
SAP S/4HANAYes (Tunnel connection)Yes
SAP BPCNoYes
SAP HANANoYes

The final workbook can be stored locally or on Microsoft SharePoint, just like a standard Excel file, and shared with other users. Both add-ins also offer the option to store workbooks in SAP Analytics Cloud and leverage its collaboration features. Additionally, SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (AFO) workbooks can be stored on SAP BW and SAP BI BusinessObjects Platform. When opening an existing workbook, both add-ins allow you to configure whether it should be automatically refreshed with new data or if manual triggering is required.

 

Analyze data

Both add-ins offer robust data analysis capabilities, including filtering through variable prompts or member selectors, ranking and sorting, hierarchical navigation, and property and totals display. Additionally, users can format data, set thresholds to highlight key values, and create Excel-based calculations within added rows or columns. Analysis for Office offers additional capabilities, such as creating custom calculations on top of data sources and defining style sets to ensure consistent formatting across workbooks. 

To enhance flexibility and customization, both add-ins support formula mode, allowing for highly adaptable and formatted reports beyond the traditional table-based layout. Furthermore, they enable data write-back formulas to planning-enabled data sources.

Due to its distinct technology, Analysis for Office offers APIs that can be utilized in VBA to automate specific functions, such as setting filters or modifying and refreshing cross-tabs. This capability is currently unavailable in Office add-ins, making it a significant limitation for migrating from AFO workbooks to SAC Excel add-in workbooks. Additionally, Analysis for Office benefits from partner solutions (APOS, BIExcellence) that can pre-calculate workbooks and distribute the results via email or as PDFs. 

 

Planning support

The SAC Excel add-in is specifically designed for SAC Planning. The planning data is directly written back into the SAP Analytics Cloud model, which can be hosted either within SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Datasphere. Additionally, workbooks can be linked to SAC calendar tasks and data actions can be triggered directly within the workbook. In an upcoming release, the add-in will also support viewing and adding data point comments, to further enhance collaboration and transparency.

Analysis for Office excels at integrating with SAP BPC and SAP BW IP Planning solutions. Comments can be stored directly within BW and Planning functions and sequences can be executed from the add-in using the side panel or VBA.

entry.title: Re: Future of SAP Landscape Management
entry.content:
Dear @mrc,

That is correct which is why we instantly updated this post at the top with the latest announcement. We didn't want to delete the previous content.

Kind regards,
Marco Dorn
Product Manager

entry.title: Re: will pMRP consider open sales order and Production or...
entry.content:
Hello 

I think those are questions that you can answer by yourself, if you properly test pMRP in the system. Try to create your own simulations, explore the different parameters, create new production orders and the observe if there is any change in the work center capacity. 

Best regards,
Caetano

entry.title: calculate version variance dynamically based on three table in SAC stories
entry.content:
Hi all,

What I want to achieve: I have three tables on my storypage. The first two tables called (trend and trend comparison, see Screenshot) have  a slider for cutoverdate and a calculated inputcontrol to select the version for the distinct table (in the left on the screenshot).My third table(called Trend variance,see screenshot) should be calculated based what displays table 1 and table 2. The logic for the values should be like this.

Table3("trend variance")= Table1 ("trend")-Table2 ("trend comparison").

Tables 1 and Table  differ in cutover date for actuals/forecast versions and selected version,

Do you have some suggestions how this is possible in SAC? My first idea was to try it with a calculated dimension based on version, but it didnt worked(screenshot 2)

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: How to use MAN_CALL to send other document than MSDS
entry.content: Thanks Chrsitophe, I will take a look deeply to config doc. I also found this note 1055007 which seems to be used full. Regards. Daman

entry.title: OR01 Tri-Met Transit Tax and OR07 Multnomah County Tax
entry.content:
We have a work location in North Portland which falls into the Tri-Met District which allows employer tax calculations for the OR01 Tri-Met Transit Tax.  Recently new hires have been assigned to OR07 Multnomah (Pre School for All, county tax), via BSI TaxProfileFactory.  This prevents the ER transit tax from calculating and causes quarterly reporting issues.

I noted a previous communication around the transit tax and reviewed note 3071303 - Multnomah County and Metro District City Oregon Taxes.  The note provides the solution to use tax authority OR31 Metro District (city tax).  The note addresses OR31 Metro District, Supportive Housing Services tax for wages earned in the city.

However, there is no reference to OR07 Multnomah which is a county tax.  This tax has a 1.5% tax rate on income above 200K.  Is there a separate solution to address this tax?

OR01  00410101 Tri-Met Trans. District (Other)
OR07  00410107 Multnomah (County)
OR31  00410109 Metro District (City)

entry.title: Re: Transport Custom Workflow from development to quality...
entry.content: I fixed the problem. It was saved as a local object (it cannot be transported to other servers). It had to be saved at a package. The problem is that now only the template gets transported without the tasks inside (there are not custom ones, its one email notification task). How can I involve them in the transport request? If you know any help would be appreciated. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Can we filter for null values in Fiori elements?
entry.content: The value help provides a datepicker and it only accepts valid dates from the calendar. there is no possibility to type 00-00-0000

entry.title: Re: SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office - Standard vs. SAP Analytics Cloud edition
entry.content:
Hi Axel,

SAC Live connections always trigger a SAC logon. Therefore it might take some more time to connect. In case connection information changes you don't need to replace this in the workbook and just change the SAC live connection. For direct connection you would need to do a system replacement in each workbook. Direct connections don't need a SAC logon and are needed for pre-calculation of workbooks. 

Regards,

Steffen

entry.title: Want to learn how to integrate SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Integrating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central with SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
The learning journey provides you with an overview of the main integration technology and implementation skills required when integrating SAP SuccessFactors with SAP S/4HANA.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to perform and configure the integration within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP Cloud Integration middleware and SAP S/4HANA as well as access the resources and support available to support the integration.

Prerequisites

  • Getting Started with SAP HCM and HCM Payroll
  • Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )

Please find the below data preview.

entry.title: Re: Transport Custom Workflow from development to quality...
entry.content:
Hi,

I would copy the standard email task to a customer task.  This should include the task into the transport request.

PS: There are a ton of threads on this site related to transporting WF task and templates.  Perhaps you should search by the SAP task that you are trying to transport (if the above approach doesn't work).

entry.title: Re: Getting error while saving section description under manage templates for performance form.
entry.content: This normally happens to us, when using images as the start of the section description (eg: as banners). I normally have to go delete a paragraph and re-enter it to be able to save. this is a recurrent issue for us as well. Manageable by alas...

entry.title: Re: Range prompt parameter hyperlink in Web Intelligence...
entry.content: Look into the Authorized URLs option for the Web Intelligence application.  This feature was added in SP3 or SP4.  This MIGHT be the issue.  Just a guess.

entry.title: Auto Packing in Public Cloud through RF Gun
entry.content:
Guru,

I am implementing SAP EWM in Public Cloud. I can do auto packing from Inbound Delivery which is in my Desktop. System will create multiple HU based on packing instruction. However I need same functionality through RF gun during Inbound process.

entry.title: Re: Range prompt parameter hyperlink in Web Intelligence...
entry.content: I've tried that but no luck. The link works fine with "lsS" single prompt. It's only an issue with the "lsR" range prompt. Not sure if there's a new format for it. In the old version the range used to be aaa..bbb (1st value, followed by two periods, followed by 2nd value)

entry.title: District field on Customer adress no translation (field CITY2)
entry.content:
Dear all

 

I am in charge of creation of new postal code / city code and district for Thailand. 

Is there possibility to key District name (field CITY2) in different language (EN and TH fo instance ? ) . 

thanks for your answer.

entry.title: Application for digital skills initiative
entry.content:
Hi  @David_Chaviano 
I was impacted by an organizational layoff couple of months ago in Canada. I believe the SAP certification under the digital skils initiative would give a new dimension to my career and help me in my future.
I identify myself under the following categories for the digital skills initiative:

  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location (Visible minority)

    Regards
    Robin

entry.title: Re: How to show the Planning Periods(Start Date,End Date) those are defined in the Version Dimension
entry.content:
@JefB I searched the Customer support tickets those were opened last November and Contacted the support team member who worked for supporting it.

The issue was resolved last November BUT API document and SAP Note clearly says it is not working officially.  

I also checked some Influence items as well, and development teams are considering to enhance  the PlanningModel Object.

At least, GetMembers issue that happened last November was resolved for supporting all customers who had used it before.

As you addressed, the API Document and NOTE says it is not supported officially but the code was fixed for supporting ONLY "GetMember of 'Version' dimension".

I believe that is the reason why Note and API document is not updated because Getmember still does not support for 'Time' Dimension.

I attached the last communication of the SAP support ticket about the issue that happened last November.

I really appreciate your detail and kind feedback. 

-----------------------------------------------------

2023-11-14 07:48:35 - *PERSON* (External Info)

Dear customer

Please find following some updates about the issue.

Regarding the unsupported feature issue in the PlanningModel.getMember() function, it has already been resolved in the following situations:


1. For QRC3 tenants, the issue is observed only with version 2023.16.16. (Roll back to version 2023.16.15 as a quick solution for the week of 2023 Nov 6. Then, this issue disappears after upgrading to QRC4 2023.21.6)
2. For QRC4 tenants, the issue is observed only with version 2023.21.5 for group 5. (Issue disappears after upgrading to version 2023.21.6)
3. Please note that these issues were reported between 2023-11-03 and 2023-11-10.

Please advise.

entry.title: Db2 odbc connection to S4 HANA in RISE
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have 3 rd party applications in azure cloud which feeds a custom table in a DB2 database. Same custom table will be synced to my ECC Db2 using federated database setup. After the sync, my ECC abap programs will use the table from the DB2 ECC server. Now we need to convert ECC to s4 in to RISE and any suggestions on how these DB2 federation can be included in the solution.

Note- We dont want any changes in the 3rd party storing methods in to staging DB.

1- in general the interfaces can be managed through pi/po/CPI in RISE. Can CPI or PI/po solutions can manage the staging Table records to sync wth S4 HANA?

2- Moving the legacy systems in to BTP services, - Very Tier some process as all the 3rd party systems need changes and no time to do this now.

3. can RISE support direct JDBC connection from onprem staging DB2 to RISE S4 HANA?

4- Can staging DB data can be synced with S/4 HANA using SLT?

5- Any other ways to manage this complex interfaces??

Please advise.

 

Rgds,

Karthik

entry.title: how to inject the clientId/secret to OAuth2UserTokenExchange destination in BTP CF using MTA.yaml
entry.content:
Hello dear community!

I am working on a project where I have a standalone app router, and several services that I want to access from my standalone sap router.

I have been able to access my UI5/services from my approuter with no issues when I create manually a destination. I want to be able to create the destination from the mta.yaml during deployment. in my mta.yaml I added the destination like this:

 

  - name: custapp-destination
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    requires:
     - name: srv-api
     - name: custapp-auth
       parameters:
        service-key:
         name: custapp-auth-key
    parameters:
      config: 
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
            - Authentication: NoAuthentication
              Name: ui5
              ProxyType: Internet
              Type: HTTP
              URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            - Authentication:  OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: custapp-api
              ProxyType: Internet
              Type: HTTP
              URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
              tokenServiceURL: https://XXX.authentication.XXX.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
              clientId: ~{custapp-auth-key}
              clientSecret: custapp-auth.clientsecret
            existing_destinations_policy: update
      service: destination
      service-plan: lite

 

unfortunately, I have been not able to find a way to dynamically determine the token serviceURL, the clientid and the clientsecret to pass it to my destination.

Is there any way to pass this values in the mta.yaml, I know that they are in the xsuaa key(custapp-auth-key).

thanks in advance for your help.

 

Tatiana F.

entry.title: Re: Plan Data Upload Starter - Directly upload plan data from a local Excel or CSV file via Story

entry.title: Notification to My Inbox Tile in S/4 Private Edition based on Condition
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!!

How to get notification in My Inbox Tile in SAP S/4 Hana Private Edition based on condition?

For Example : A Purchase order got created with Amount 3 Lakhs. so here requirement is to get notification in My Inbox Tile if amount of PO is more that 2 lakhs.

Thanks in Advance

Regards,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: Re: GR and GI Areas for Multiple Plants Linked to a Singl...
entry.content:
Hi Sachndev,

In the destination Goods Issue (GI) area for a particular plant during picking, or the Goods Receipt (GR) area, can be determined based on the setup of your warehouse task. The warehouse task contains information about the source and destination storage bins for the goods movement.

When you create a warehouse task for picking, the system determines the source storage bin (where the goods are currently stored) and the destination storage bin (where the goods need to be moved to) based on the warehouse layout and the settings in your warehouse management master data. For each plant, you can define separate storage types for GI and GR areas.

When you create a transfer order for picking, the system will determine the destination GI area based on the storage type and storage bin information in the warehouse task. Similarly, for goods receipt, the system will determine the GR area based on the storage type and storage bin information in the warehouse task.

Please note that the exact determination of GI and GR areas can depend on various factors such as the warehouse structure, the configuration of your storage types and storage bins, and the settings in your warehouse management master data.

Please let us know if you have further questions by here.

Best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Re: MTA Standalone Approuter with UI5 Tooling, live reload?
entry.content: Hi Tristan, any idea on how to do this with a typescript app? We had a similar solution before changing to typescript but now we require the extra build step...

entry.title: Talent Modules Implementation withouth EC
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have a new client who is considering moving from their legacy HRIS System to Successfactors and they wouldl like to consider the below two escenarios:

1. Implementing talent modules with EC (I don't think it maybe any issue)

2. Implementing talent moduls without EC

But for this esceario I have been looking there are some challengues as some functionalities are not going to be available even if the client implement the EC mini master.

according to the kba 3340964 - Onboarding and EC Mini Limitations (sap.com)

  • EC should not be used for core HR, like hire transfer promotions etc.. For all these events the data comes from the customers core HR systems. My understand is we can not use ONB 2.0 to transfer the information to Employee profile directly, is that understanding correct?
  • Crossboarding and Offboarding are not supported with mini EC.
  • No Manager or Employee Self Service. Neither for functionalities that only applies for employee profile?
  • Position Management should NOT be used in EC.  
  • My question is Succession Management we can nominated people through MDF Position nomination method without EC, so those it mean I cannot use the integration with RCM because recruitment will not have access to this position object.
  • Can we have something similar in RCM to manage the position hierarchy?
  • Comp. calculations should also not be used. My question is what does it mean calculations shoudl also not be used, what calculations? calculations that involves EC fields for  compensation plan?
  • The EC functions are read only from the UI.

Any other considerations?

Looking forward to your response 

Regards

entry.title: S/4HANA service - requote a service quation with reference to maintenance order
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have some issue with new service s/4hana.

In new S/4HANA service with Advanced Execution we have an Service Quotation (SQ) 4000000184.

SQ has a two main items.

  • 10) Execution item  - this item generates maintenance order, where a costs are calculated.
    • 13) spare part
    • 16) work hours
  • 20) Expanse item 

SQ 4000000184:

10) execution item has reference to maintenance order  4000803

 

The next step, after the customer accepted the SQ,  the system generate follow-up document - Service order

Until now everything is ok.

But when we want a requote SQ(4000000184),  system creates a new SQ (4000000185), but wihout reference to maintenace order. So subitems are missing.

 

SQ 4000000185

Execetion order item - item. no. 10 doesn't have any reference to maintenance order.

 

 

 

Is this a normal state?  Or do you have any workaround for that? Because price details doesn't work properly without subitems. The New SQ (4000000185) should have a reference to maintenance order 4000803.

 

Tested on S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

 

Thanks you for your help.

Vojta

entry.title: Re: Migrating Applications from XSJS to Async-XSJS
entry.content:
Hi Sg81,

HANA XSA is good to go with CAP as well. I strongly recommend to move away from the XSJS side. Also on HANA 2 this is the better way to go ahead. You can use BTP tool Business Application Studio to work on HANA 2 as well. With CAP you can migrate from HANA 2 to HANA Cloud sooner or later. But you have modern tools with expertise in the field.

 

BR Volker

entry.title: 🟠 SAP Fiori Tools - preview-middleware
entry.content:
In this presentation we will discuss the benefits of using the preview-middleware to reduce the amount of glue code required to locally run a UI5 app in a sandbox launchpad. Additionally, we will explore how this middleware can streamline the process of running QUnit and OPA5 tests, improving efficiency and maintainability.

tl;dr: We're going to delete things.

entry.title: Re: Migrating Applications from XSJS to Async-XSJS
entry.content: Thanks a lot for quick response. So you are suggesting to use BAS for moving to CAP/CDS format and keeping HANA 2.0 for later migration to HANA cloud. Any documents/blogs available to help with CAP on HANA 2.0 using BAS? I will not be able to leverage CF services, might need to keep using XS services e.g. job scheduler, destinations etc. kindly let me know.

entry.title: Re: Sourcing Project Template in Guided sourcing
entry.content: For me Ariba told that it is the expected behavior. Also i dont see any documentation related to this in Help sap portal.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: Using SAP SuccessFactors solutions as an Event Sour
entry.content: Very neat and straight forward...!

entry.title: 🟠 What’s new in SAP Fiori elements – demos and live coding
entry.content: Experience the latest innovations from the SAP Fiori elements team. In this session we will focus on live demos and demonstrate the latest and greatest of the SAP Fiori elements & SAP Fiori tools framework (e.g. tree table, collaborative draft) including our achievements in Artificial Intelligence (smart summarization, easy filter).

entry.title: Re: Nuevo subsidio al empleo 2024 Mexico
entry.content: 3470041_New Tax Subsidy for Employees Decree 2024 + 3295584_TAX New configuration option + 3473499_Correction to Decree - 2024 subsidy for employees + 2894372_Monthly tax adjustment by userexits

entry.title: Re: Custom Value help on standard Fiori application using...
entry.content:
Hi,

The way I solved a similar requirement was by creating the Custom Field but DO NOT SELECT "Code list with CDS view".

Then, search for the CDS View where your custom field was added and then create a metadata extension. There you can add your own Value Help.

In my case I was asked to add a Value Help to a Custom field, but the value help should filter data based on other custom field:

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
annotate entity C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP
  with 
{
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [
    {
      entity: {
          name:     'ZVE_I_RISKS',
          element:  'RiskName'
      },
      additionalBinding: [{ element:      'Country', 
                            localElement: 'ZZCountry' }]
    }
  ]  
  ZZ1_Risk_Name_NTF;
  
}

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM - Can a Transport of Copy be Required?
entry.content:
Hi,
At this point, the creation of Transport of Copies is optional.  

I have not heard any plans to enforce a ToC creation requirement, to this point.

If you would like to request such a requirement in Cloud ALM's Change and Deployment Management, you can add an entry in the Influencer Campaign:

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525
Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Adding / changing printer for Manage Physical Inventory Documents app
entry.content:
With release 2408 upgrade S/4HANA deleted Print Physical Inventory Document app replacing it with Manage Physical Inventory Documents app, how to we assign same out queue/printer as deleted app to new app.

I tried using Output Parameter Determination app, but with my very limited SAP knowledge I am not able to find out how to assign printer or printq to Manage Physical Inventory Documents app.

Thanks in advance for your help

Ashraf

S/4HANA Public Cloud

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien, um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden!

entry.title: Re: How to reset your S-user password
entry.content:
Hi ranjan_sharma!

Sorry the delay!

We Checked SAP Note 2711450  and many others but our customer give up due to time needed to investigate and test.

SAP Library About Password policies:

https://help.sap.com/viewer/6d6d63354d1242d185ab4830fc04feb1/Cloud/en-US/12b33953a9164b6084319f4785808a8e.html

To check the current authenticated S-USER policies:

https://accounts.sap.com/ui/protected/userData

Regards, CD

entry.title: Which SAP CAL appliance is best suited for installing SAP ECC system in AWS cloud?
entry.content:
Greetings, community

My goal is to launch and use a SAP ECC 6.0 system on the AWS cloud.

I wanted to know if SAP Cloud Appliance Library (SAP CAL) could be used for that. If so, please advise which appliance template I should use to do the same thing.

Please advise which SAP licenses I should get as well.

I want to perform test and demo using such SAP ECC 6.0 system.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Indian DPDP Act's Impact on SAP Customers: Navigating Data Privacy Compliance
entry.content:
Hi Raghu,

This is very informative blog is there any solution that SAP can deploy in its individual customers with SAP ECC6.0 version ? For example if SAP server is operated outside of India but transactions does happens for india business. In this case in order to follow compliance rules what are the possible solutions we can have with SAP. Also if the SAP is in S4 version do we have a different approach than SAP ECC6.0.

Thank You,

Venkat C

entry.title: It's All About The People – SAP Social Sabbatical in Lusaka, Zambia 2024
entry.content:
A few weeks ago, twelve SAP employees from around the world set out on their journeys to Lusaka, Zambia as part of SAP’s Corporate Social Responsibility’s flagship pro-bono consulting program, the SAP Social Sabbatical (SoSa).  

For the four-week duration of the program they provided sustainable support for the four social enterprises they had been paired with, experiencing the Zambian culture and country and forging lasting friendships from the people they met. 
 
Meet Vitalite 

Vitalite Zambia is a social enterprise that provides solar home systems and efficient appliances to rural and peri-urban communities in Zambia. Its mission is to positively impact lives and livelihoods by making quality products, services, and renewable energy accessible and affordable to all Zambian households.  

SAP consultants Thais Druwe, Om Band and Tobias Hiob were tasked with providing a foundation for streamlining Vitalite’s operations, enabling it to grow revenues and ensure that its operational processes were scalable. Their scope of work spanned from creating a policy guideline to improve processes for National Sales and Services and providing general business advice to drive further scalability and growth.  

While members of the social enterprise learned from them, the four weeks also offered valuable lessons for the SAP team. These included the benefit of ‘company dance-offs’ in establishing a positive culture, Zambians’ openness and positive mindset, the importance of putting the customer first and the true magic of creating trust in the team and how complementary strengths can go a long way. 

As the SAP team noted, “our sabbatical with VITALITE Zambia re-invigorated our spirit to leverage business for good. It demonstrated how enterprises can create lasting community impacts and motivated a renewed focus on making a societal difference.” 

Meet Genius Education 

Genius Education Zambia is an EdTech social enterprise that exists at the intersection of science, education and leadership. Genius Education aims to facilitate, enhance and promote quality emerging STEM education by empowering the Zambian education system.  

But despite six years of operations, Genius Education lacked essential financial systems, hindering its ability to launch social business ventures for sustainability and achieve socio-economic impact in Zambia. Therefore, the scope of work for the SoSa team was to make the social enterprise’s financial processes future-ready by implementing a financial system, conducting training and creating documentation. To ensure long-term success, a post-program implementation plan was introduced to cover ongoing support and outline a strategic financial model.  

The SAP consultants Brian Vance, Julia Hasslinger and Kate Yambao worked hand-in-hand with the young and dynamic team from Genius making this a truly unique experience. The team of three said afterwards that Zambia was, “easily as the most welcoming culture we've ever encountered,” and that they experienced this every day at Genius. ” We know we will be forever changed by this experience and we're so grateful for the learning, the sharing, the kindness, and the way (the people at) Genius opened their arms to us. At the end of the 4-week project, we’ve not only delivered our scope of work, but the greatest milestone is gaining a family at Genius Education Zambia.” 

Meet Ebusaka 

Ebusaka is a social enterprise that helps connect the many stakeholders in waste management with their app to help inspire cleaner cities starting with the capital, Lusaka.  

The SAP consultant team, Dekel Liberman and Rana Hamzakadi took on the task to help the green tech start up implement project management and team processes, introduce branding and social media planning and support the Ebusaka team with an onsite media launch event that resulted in increased visibility in its target market. Ebusaka’s founders Muyunda and Michael opened their home to the team to conduct workshops and provide the consultants with a glimpse of what it takes to inspire clean cities in Zambia.  

With only 10% of the country’s waste being properly collected and processed, there is still a lot to do in the Zambian waste management industry. And it takes inspirational leaders like Muyunda, who are ready to take on the challenge to shift towards a more sustainable and cleaner future.

Meet BongoHive 

BongoHive is Zambia’s first tech and innovation hub which aims to assist scalable startups by providing a forum for the exchange of ideas and reduce the barriers to entrepreneurship. 

To support BongoHive with making Zambia Africa’s next hotspot of innovation, SAP consultants, Suvarna BH, Mikhail Fedorov, and Eugenio Presno immersed themselves in BongoHive’s operations, exploring its mission, vision and efforts to empower the entrepreneurship ecosystem. The team of three offered their insights and perspectives that helped the organization influence its approach to creating impactful products and services. 

“From brainstorming sessions to hands-on project work, their dedication and enthusiasm were palpable, inspiring our different units and igniting new ideas for future endeavors,” said Lukonga Lindunda, CEO of BongoHive in a recent LinkedIn post as they bid farewell to the SAP team. “Their 

contributions have left an indelible mark on our organization, propelling us forward on our journey towards greater innovation and social impact.”  

It’s All About The People 

“It was inspiring to see the genuine dedication to doing good within this cohort, as they came together with a shared focus on making positive impact." shared team mentor Elitsa. 

Reflecting on his experience with BongoHive, Eugenio Presno said, “sometimes, when submerged in the day-to-day never-ending list of things to do both for work and for my family, it is easy to lose sight of what is important in life. This month has given me the chance to put my life and the lives of the people I care about in a different perspective.” He added, "In just four weeks, I have met, worked, and exchanged ideas with so many people that are trying to make this world a better place.... in Zambia I have found such passion, such energy to do good for children in schools, for the environment, for entrepreneurs with big dreams, or for people that just with a couple of lightbulbs are able to improve their lives so profoundly.” 

Thanks to the #SAPSocialSabbatical consultants: Julia, Kate, Mikhail, Suvarna, Eugenio, Tobias, Om, Thais, Brian and Steffen, our amazing team mentor Elitsa and project managers from Pyxera Global: Nicholas, Daniela and Peter. 

To learn more about the SAP Social Sabbatical program, check out #SAPSocialSabbatical 

#LifeAtSAP

entry.title: Re: locked for automatic sourcing
entry.content:
This must be by design of SAP, if the material version is  not released then you can have MRP run it to create the PR and then PO automatically. SAP Intent may be not to run the MRP can create a procurement proposal for product version that not fully approved to be purchased or ready to be purchased as part of regular procurement process hence it have option to stop automatic sourcing indicator for the version.

Automatic Sourcing allow to assign of info records as a source of supply. I guess you have to activate plant level info record if you planning to use the version field on info records.

entry.title: Re: Error occurred during creation of purchase requisitio...
entry.content:
Please check SAP note -- and make sure a combination of F and item category L is allowed in the configuration 

Materials Management-->Purchasing-->Account Assignment-->Define Combination of Item Categories/Account Assignment Categories

3267711 - Purchase Requisition for external Operation Sub-contracting not created - SAP for Me

entry.title: UDF in Landed Costs is not showing up in Service Layer
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am having issue with user-defined fields created in SAP B1 but they are not being displayed in service layer metadata (Landed Costs). As a result of this, I cannot see the data when I invoke the service layer endpoint.

Please assist me what the error could me.

I have SAP B1 9.3 PL09

Thanks

entry.title: sortProperty and filterProperty not working, but column is visible in Columns tab in p13n dialog
entry.content:
I have the following code in my view.xml =>

 

 

 

<table:Column id="TimeBucketCol00" hAlign="End" minWidth="100">
                            <Label text="{i18n>invoiceDescription}" />
                            <table:customData>
                                <core:CustomData key="p13nData" value='\{"columnKey": "invoiceDescription", "leadingProperty":"invoiceDescription", "sortProperty": "invoiceDescription", "filterProperty": "invoiceDescription", "type":"string"}'/>
                            </table:customData>
                            <table:template>
                                <Text text="{invoiceDescription}" wrapping="false"/>
                            </table:template>
                        </table:Column>

 

The metadata =>

<Property Name="invoiceDescription" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="255"/>

 

 

 However once I open the p13n dialog, I don't find the column in my sort/filter tab

 



Once I remove it from my customData then the filter/sort appears, but then I want to have formatter on that column, which is why I used customData tag.

Is there a way to -> 
1. Add formatter on my backend, which uses CDS annotations
2. Also, if i use customData on a column does it override whatever annotations are given for that column in the backend?
3. What changes should I do in my above code?

entry.content: OIISLVCPO

Saminder

entry.title: BAdI: how to know what they are for without dev knowledge?
entry.content:
Dear community,

Points will be awarded to the best answer. Context: I'm a functional PP and QM SAP S/4 functional consultant, I do not know anything about code/ABAP.

Question: How to know what BAdI is best in the SE38 tcode for any requirement? I know where to find them, but how can I start understanding them as a functional consultant without coding expertise?

Challenge: Utilizing the newest Fiori Extensibility for Custom Logic would be awesome, but I'm limited until I figure out how to find the proper BAdI. 

 

Thank you!

Luis

entry.title: Re: locked for automatic sourcing
entry.content: Hello, the automatic sourcing in the info record is not related to the production order.

entry.title: Saving SAP ENABLE NOW SCORM file in a lower version
entry.content: Our LMS system only accepts SCORM files in version 1.2.  Our current version of SAP ENABLE NOW only saves in version 1.3.3.  How do we save the SCORM file in a lower version for the LMS system?

entry.title: SAP Best Practices for Transportation Management
entry.content:
SAP TM Implementation Template

In my previous blog, I covered some of the business benefits of using Best Practices for Logistics and using templates available from SAP.

In this blog, I will highlight the Best Practices available for SAP S/4HANA TM, focusing on how templates have sped up the implementation process of SAP products. 

The points I will cover include: 

  • What's in the Best Practice Content?
  • Where to find the Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA TM?

These are some of the business benefits for using Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA TM:

  • A guide for End to End Transportation processes
  • Accelerated approach to Inbound, Outbound and STO processing
  • Reduced time spent defining transportation master data
  • Standardizing transportation processes across your the business 

 

Simplifying the Movement of Goods 

 

What's in the Best Practice Content?

Below is the process flow for a Stock Transport Order across ERP, warehousing and transportation management. The process flows available are extremely detailed and include user roles as well. The Best Practice content for Transportation Management has the 3 main Business Processes in transportation which are Inbound, Outbound and moving stock between plants(Stock Transport Order). A fourth Best Practice is also available for the combination of Inbound and Outbound orders using integrated Transportation Management.

 

 

STO TM

 

 

  • Process Flows

A detailed process flow is available across ERP and TM with user roles, this is extremely useful to customers that want to standardise their processes. 

  • Accelerators

Test scripts are delivered to guide unit testing and end-user testing. 

  • Roles

Predefined Roles are available for all warehouse processes, this takes reduces the workload during implementation of authorization team.

  • Master Data, Organizational Data, and Other Data

The organizational structure reflects the structure of an example company. The master data represents materials, customers, and suppliers, depending on the operational focus of the demonstrated processes. The business process is enabled with this organization-specific master data.

Where to find the Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA TM?

The Best Practice content can be found at SAP4ME. This is a link to the main content for SAP S/4HANA 1909. These notes can be used to help you understand the the content better and what is needed to get started. 

Collective note 2970208:
SAP Best Practices for S/4HANA Supply Chain for Transportation Management 1909 content of all released notes below:

SAP Best Practices for S/4HANA Supply Chain for Transportation Management

Outbound Transportation (Basic Shipping Scenario)
2969153

Stock Transport Orders (Basic Shipping Scenario)
2970165
Inbound Transportation (Basic Shipping Scenario)
2970146

Combination of Inbound and Outbound Transportation (Integrated Transportation Management) 2970191

Conclusion

The Best Practice content for SAP S/4HANA TM is available for customers with a valid license which cover the major business processes for most Shipping companies across different industries. SAP is continuously investing in Best Practice content to simplify implementations.

I hope this blog helped will help reduce the effort to implement SAP TM. If you have any questions, please feel free to post it below or leave a comment.
In my next blog I plan to give an overview of the role of Business Networks in Logistics. 

For more updates on Transportation Management please visit:

SAP TM community


If you have any questions on Transportation Management please visit
https://answers.sap.com/index.html
 
SAP Learning
SAP TM Learning Journey

Hi @hristotsolev ,

entry.title: Re: New Integration of SAP Cloud Transport Management and SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Thank you for the blog Moritz!

For cloud Integration transports, do the deployment happen automatically with feature status change? Like when we confirm successful test , will this import the TR in the subsequent tenant? or do we have to manually go to the CTMS tenant queue and import it?

entry.title: Re: Looking for the best way to calculate inflation
entry.content:
@GhouriM 

I think the first approach is much cleaner in case you get inflation affecting more accounts. In second approach you will end up creating too many accounts. Having that extra source dimension will give you flexibility to model other requirements also. Just 2 thoughts on this approach, You could just use a single account say Acc1 to store all data and use source members Fcst/Inflation/Adjustment to store the different values. Also I would suggest that you store the final result post inflation in another source member for cleaner auditability of results else post inflation effect you will never be able to trace back original forecast value and if you run the calculation multiple times you will end up applying 5 % inflation on each run. Also you could separate the inflation % input and the inflation amount 50 on a different source. It will really help in Data reconciliation when you are analyzing this calculation at a later stage for multiple period and other combination.

Something like below.

MEMBERSET [D/ACCOUNT]="acc1"

MEMBERSET[D/SOURCE]=("FCST","ADJ","INF_INP","INF","FINAL_FSCT")

DATA(D/SOURCE]="INF')=RESULTLOOKUP(D/SOURCE]="INF_INP")*RESULTLOOKUP(D/SOURCE]="FCST")

DATA(D/SOURCE]="FINAL_FSCT')=RESULTLOOKUP(D/SOURCE]="FSCT")+RESULTLOOKUP(D/SOURCE]="INF")-RESULTLOOKUP(D/SOURCE]="ADJ")

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Custom Shell plugin in Mobile start not visible
entry.content:
I have developed a simple shell plugin to open an app using semantic/action. Its is visible in WZ standard edition Desktop browser and works as expected, but not in mobile device. is it limitation or do I miss any configuration ?

 
Component.js
 
 oRenderer.addHeaderEndItem({
                        icon: "sap-icon://manager",
                        tooltip: "My Substitutes",
                        target: "#ZMANAGE-create"
                    }, true, false);
Manifest
  "hideLauncher": true,
  "semanticObject": "Shell",
  "action": "plugin"
....
"sap.flp": {
    "type": "plugin"
  },
.....
 "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
 
Desktop:

 Mobile Device (Mobile Start app)

Any pointers would help

Thanks,

Vijay

entry.title: Re: Single selection in hierarchal input filter - SAC
entry.content:
@Deb_D 

May be 2 filters with cascading on. First with the group that is Capex and R&D and second filter for projects which should filter based on group filter selected first.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP TM, Subcontracting email to the carrier has wrong...
entry.content:
Please check this when you get chance.

  • Communication Party

    If you enter a communication party in your freight order or freight booking and choose Send to Carrier (freight order) or Send Booking (freight booking), the system sends messages to this communication party instead of to the carrier. In tendering, if you have assigned a communication party to a carrier in the business partner master data, the system sends messages (for example, B2B messages, e-mails, or text messages) to the contact person of the communication party and not the contact person of the carrier.

entry.title: Re: How to show the Planning Periods(Start Date,End Date) those are defined in the Version Dimension
entry.content:
Thank you for digging into it more @james_lim
Let's hope for more clarity on the official path forward for increased API support.

entry.title: Re: Convert Binary number to Hex
entry.content: You Save My Day. Thanks !

entry.title: Re: How to show the Planning Periods(Start Date,End Date) those are defined in the Version Dimension
entry.content:
@james_lim @JefB 

I agree with Jef here as we were also the ones affected and the point is at that point SAP said that we should not have used an unsupported API even though it worked and also advised to move away with this approach. The reason they supported was because of sheer quantum of disruption it created as every other customer had used the API for similar requirements especially around version dimension. This also brings up the point that reading a simple thing as dimension attribute value should not have required scripting in first place. There should be standard API's or method which should return the property value with dimension member as a input parameter. Even the good old BPC had this feature 10 years back. 

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: In ALV When clicked on button of a cell, other cell populate some value (SY-DATUM)
entry.content: SY-DATUM, not SY-DATE. Transfer the date to the internal table and refresh the ALV.

entry.title: Re: Express Document "update was cancelled" received from...
entry.content: I have changed the new number range with material type but still showing same error.

entry.content:
@SteffenHeine 

Thanks for this. It helps in comparing the key features of the Excel based offerings. Could you help in understanding the below w.r.t SAC Excel Add-in

Both add-ins also offer the option to store workbooks in SAP Analytics Cloud and leverage its collaboration features.

How can we store Excel Add-in based template or reports in SAC tenant as a central storage and allow access like a platform.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: PO item, Order( internal order) fields are missing in...
entry.content: After activating the System varaints shown below these two fields are reflecting in SO line item wise.

entry.title: Re: How to make field optional to mandatory in Import Sal...
entry.content:
Hello Rahul,

Yes, you can make this field mandatory.

Through configuration activity 'Configure Sales Document Types' (ID: 102495), you can select the desired Document Type, i.e. 'OR', and maintain the field 'Reference Mandatory'.

Doing that, the field will not be marked as required on the import template, but when you do the import it will warn you that a reference document is mandatory and the import will fail if it's missing.

Kind regards,
Luiza.

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer between the company codes (Intercompany Asset Transfer)
entry.content:
Hi Syed, nice and well explained blog!

One thing I want to know is: if both the company codes use cross-company number ranges, can the Asset be transferred with the same Asset number to the receiving company code as in the sender?

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 22nd, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/22/sap-developer-news-august-22nd-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP CodeJams LATAM Wrap Up

  • Request your own SAP CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415

ABAP CDS 2408 Release & External Entities

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/technology-blog-sap/article-id/174427
  • SAP Help Portal  Link - https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-data-models/cds-external-entities?version=s4hana_cloud

SAP TechEd Session Catalog is Live!

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

SAP Open Source Licenses and AI Webinar

  • Registration links: internal / external participants

sap.ui.mdc library available for productive usage

  • Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/smart-and-flexible-sap-ui-mdc-released-for-productive-use/ba-p/13793439
  • Samples: https://ui5.sap.com/#/entity/sap.ui.mdc/sample/sap.ui.mdc.demokit.sample.TableFilterBarJson
  • Tutorial: https://github.com/SAP-samples/ui5-mdc-json-tutorial

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP CodeJams LATAM Wrap Up

1:19 ABAP CDS Release News 2408 – External Entities

2:58 TechEd Session Catalog is Live

3:37 SAP Open Source Licenses and AI Webinar

4:33 sap.ui.mdc library available for productive usage

Transcript

[Antonio and Josh] Hola, SAP developers. Hey, everybody. It's Josh. And Antonio. We are so pleased with the attendance, participation, and the feedback from these eight, ocho, CodeJams we just finished yesterday. Exactly. So from Sao Paulo, Santiago, Buenos Aires, and Bolotá, we experienced four great days focused on event-driven integrations and another four great days focused on SAP Build Apps, and a few Stammtisch as well. Besides the amazing technology and the people, the locations, I also learned something new. We traveled all over another continent. My first time here in South America, Latown, but I felt so at home. We talked SAP, shared community stories, and we chatted about Devtoberfest and TechEd. Agreed. I was quite happy to get to meet many developers around here and colleagues in this region as well. So I'm looking forward to meeting more in the near future. Yep. So that's it for this road trip. Thank you all so much. Muchas gracias. See you next time. Y lazamos, muchos gracias. Ciao.

[Sheena] The standard database of application server ABAP is SAP HANA database. And SAP ABAP program requires access to another database we use to configure a secondary connection. Andrea Schlotter, in her latest blog post on the 24.08 release of ABAP CDS, explains a new and exciting feature, CDS external entities. CDS external entities help to retrieve data from multiple other remote sources, which can be SAP HANA or non-SAP HANA databases. Unlike traditional secondary connections that limit access to a single database and require exact matching of remote and local objects, CDS external entities offer a more flexible and efficient approach by allowing access to multiple remote data sources within a single SQL statement. This feature leverages the data federation approach of the SAP HANA Smart Data Access technology. The blog post also talks about the comparison of secondary connection and external entities, how to access data in ABAP SQL, and establish outbound SQL access using an external entity. Please note that currently, only dynamic external entities are supported, which can only be accessed using ABAP SQL and cannot be used as a data source for other CDS entities. Also note that the dynamic external entities allow only read access, and writable external entities are planned for one of the upcoming releases. For details, check out the links in the description.

[Tom] Planning for TechEd Virtual 2024 is officially in full swing. And I'm thrilled to report that the session catalog is now live. Now is the time to explore all of the learning opportunities we have in store during our free virtual event this October. You'll find over 90 live sessions, including live lectures, code demos, track overviews, jumpstart tutorials, and expert analysis. Signing up is simple, just visit sapteched.com and register for our free virtual event on October 8th and 9th. So sign up now and I'll see you online in October.

[Ajay] Hello, SAP developers, greetings of the day. Generative AI, LLMs, tools such as Copilot and Codestral, everybody is in it now. But what does it mean in terms of responsibilities in the daily work of a developer in an enterprise context? SAP Open Source Webinar on Topic, Open Source Licenses within the context of AI is available on September 3rd, 2024 at 3 p.m. CEST. Georgi, who is an open source champion at SAP, will give an overview of illegal environment surrounding AI, talk about restrictions and obligations of licenses used for AI components, and give you examples of technical and regulatory solutions to avoid IP infringements. If this sounds interesting to you, do not miss this opportunity. Links to the registrations are in the description below.

[Nico] Hi, everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. The SAP UI MDC library is now available for productive usage, and this is since SAPUI5 and OpenUI5 version 1.124. The MDC controls, which is short for metadata-driven controls, by the way, help you build smart controls and even whole applications following the SAP Fiori design guidelines. The SAP Fiori Elements flexible programming model for RoadAW4 also use these controls under the hood. But they work with other protocols such as plain REST as well. Check out these samples in the UI5 documentation, as well as the corresponding tutorial on GitHub. Links are in the description, and I hope to see you soon.

entry.title: Re: BKPF-XBLNR (Reference Field is not changeable)
entry.content: @sanilbhandari -
